skill.name: AI Image Generation — verging.ai
skill.description: Generate AI images using DALL-E 3 / gpt-image-1 through the verging.ai proxy API.
skill.summary: Generate AI images using DALL-E 3 and gpt-image-1 through the verging.ai proxy API with standard or HD quality.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only, providing instructions for using the verging.ai API to generate AI images. The 14 static findings are all false positives: bash code blocks were misidentified as Ruby execution, public API URLs were flagged as suspicious, and an API key placeholder was mistaken for a hardcoded secret. There is no executable code in the skill — only README and SKILL.md markdown files. The required user actions (setting an API key, making API requests) are standard and expected for any third-party API integration skill.

low_findings.title: External API dependency
low_findings.description: The skill requires users to obtain an API key from verging.ai and make requests to https://verging.ai/api/v1/ai/imagegen. This is a third-party paid service. Users should review the verging.ai terms of service and pricing before use.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with DALL-E and GPT-Image-1
content.value_statement: Creating images with AI requires managing API keys, request parameters, and response handling. This skill provides a ready-to-use interface to generate images through the verging.ai proxy API, supporting both standard and HD quality modes.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using DALL-E 3 or gpt-image-1 models
Support both standard (2 credits) and HD (3 credits) quality modes
Generate 1 to 4 images per single API request
Return CDN-hosted image URLs for easy download and sharing
Document error codes and rate limit handling for production use
limitations: Requires a verging.ai account and valid API key with credits
Limited to 1024x1024 default resolution with no custom size options documented
No batch processing or asynchronous job support documented
Dependent on verging.ai service availability and upstream provider uptime

use_cases.title: Marketing and blog imagery
use_cases.description: Generate unique illustrations and header images for blog posts, social media, and marketing materials without sourcing stock photos.

use_cases.title: Product mockups and concepts
use_cases.description: Create visual concepts and mockups for product ideas, landing pages, and design explorations during early-stage planning.

use_cases.title: Creative writing illustrations
use_cases.description: Generate scene illustrations to accompany fiction, game narratives, or storytelling projects.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image using verging.ai with the prompt: "A serene mountain lake at dawn, photorealistic style"
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Quick image generation with minimal parameters

prompt_templates.title: Generate HD quality image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an HD quality image via verging.ai: prompt is "Modern minimalist logo for a tech startup, vector style", quality set to "hd", size 1024x1024
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Higher quality output for professional use

prompt_templates.title: Generate multiple variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 image variations via verging.ai with prompt "Futuristic cityscape at night, cyberpunk neon style", quality standard, n=4 to get options
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Multiple outputs for comparison and selection

prompt_templates.title: Custom model selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate image via verging.ai using model "dall-e-3" with prompt "Vintage botanical illustration of roses, watercolor texture", quality hd
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: Explicit model selection for specific style requirements

output_examples.input: Generate an image: "A futuristic cityscape at sunset, digital art"
output_examples.output: The API returns a JSON response with an array of CDN-hosted image URLs and credit consumption information. The image would be available at the returned URL for download or embedding. Standard quality costs 2 credits, HD costs 3 credits per image.

output_examples.input: Generate 2 HD images: "Mountain landscape, oil painting style"
output_examples.output: The response includes two CDN URLs for the generated images and reports total credits consumed (6 credits for 2 HD images). Each URL points to a PNG file on verging.ai's CDN ready for use.

best_practices: Store your API key in an environment variable and never commit it to version control or share it publicly
Start with standard quality for testing and only use HD quality for final production assets to conserve credits
Write descriptive prompts with style, lighting, and composition details for better results
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode your VERGING_API_KEY in scripts or configuration files that get committed to repositories
Do not request HD quality for every image during testing or iteration phases as it consumes credits quickly
Do not use vague single-word prompts like "dog" or "house" — they produce low-quality generic results

faq.question: What models are available for image generation?
faq.answer: The verging.ai API supports DALL-E 3 and gpt-image-1 models. The default model is gpt-image-1.

faq.question: How much does each image cost?
faq.answer: Standard quality images cost 2 credits each. HD quality images cost 3 credits each. Generating multiple images multiplies the cost accordingly.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and select API Keys to create a new key. Then set it as the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What image sizes are supported?
faq.answer: The default resolution is 1024x1024 pixels. The size parameter accepts string values for image dimensions.

faq.question: How many images can I generate per request?
faq.answer: You can request 1 to 4 images per API call using the n parameter. The default is 1.

faq.question: What happens when I run out of credits?
faq.answer: The API returns HTTP 402 with error code 40001 indicating insufficient credits. You need to top up your verging.ai account to continue generating images.
